Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets list typical sequence: 1. Receive Order (RO) 2. Check Availability (CA) 3. Reserve Items (RI) 4. Pick Items (PI) 5. Quality Check (QC) 6. Pack Order (PO) 7. Generate Shipping Label (GSL) 8. Dispatch (D) 9. Invoice Customer (IC) 10. Record Payment (RP)

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: activity occurrences: We need to set bounds for each activity. Typically each activity occurs exactly once per case. So we can set {1} for each. activ_freq: {'RO': {1}, 'CA': {1}, 'RI': {1}, 'PI': {1}, 'QC': {1}, 'PO': {1}, 'GSL': {1}, 'D': {1}, 'IC': {1}, 'RP': {1}}

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: equivalence: Two activities that always occur the same number of times. For example Reserve Items and Pick Items? ... Given that all activities occur exactly once, any pair would satisfy that. But we wouldnt list all pairs. Typically equivalence is used for activities that are alternative or parallel. So maybe leave equivalence empty

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: never_together: Activities that cannot co-exist in the same case. Possibly none? No never_together constraints

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: directly_follows: RO -> CA, CA -> RI, RI -> PI, PI -> QC, QC -> PO, PO -> GSL, GSL -> D, D -> IC, IC -> RP. directly_follows set: {('RO','CA'), ('CA','RI'), ... ('IC','RP')}

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: always_before: If first occurs, then second must have occurred previously. So for D, all earlier: RO, CA, RI, PI, QC, PO, GSL. (D, RO), (D, CA), ... For each later activity, all earlier activities

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: always_after: If first occurs, then second occurs later. So (RO, CA), (CA, RI), (RI, PI), ... all pairs where first is earlier, second later

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Do we need to include all always_before pairs? That would be many 45 pairs. Maybe only immediate? Discusses redundancy with directly_follows and frequencies. But decides to include all because they hold

Name: Validation - PE
Text: With frequencies {1} and directly_follows for total order, enforces exact sequence. always_before and always_after also hold and add checks. equivalence empty, never_together empty

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: log_skeleton = { 'equivalence': set(), 'always_before': set([('CA', 'RO'), ... ]), 'always_after': set([('RO', 'CA'), ... ]), 'never_together': set(), 'activ_freq': {'RO': {1}, ...}, 'directly_follows': set([('RO', 'CA'), ...]) }
